About Me

Engineer. Designer. Creator.

I’m a Honors Mechanical Engineering student at Texas A&M University with a 3.93 GPA and Minors in Physics & Mathematics. I love to be at the intersection of coursework theory, system design, and technical hands-on creation — whether that’s designing CAD models in SolidWorks, fabricating with metal and polymers, or writing simulation code in Python.

Outside of academics, I’m the founder of RAD Builds™, a custom prop-building business and YouTube channel with 1,000+ subscribers where I teach others how to create high-quality props. I am also a driving member in our multdisciplinary project team creating EcoFil, a 3-phase 3D printing filament recycler from scratch, in Aggies Create. I’ve also studied engineering economics abroad in Greece and competed in engineering design challenges through the Texas A&M’s Student Chapter of the American Society of Mechanical Engineers (ASME).

I’m driven by curiosity, craftsmanship, and the challenge of turning an idea on paper into something real.

Projects

Engineering & Creative Work

A selection of academic, research, and personal projects spanning mechanical design, software simulation, fabrication, and creative building.

Current · Research

EcoFil – 3D Printing Recycler

Collaborating on a 5-person team to design a closed-loop 3-phase recycling system converting 3D printing waste into reusable filament: shredding, dehydration, and thermal re-extrusion. Developed 15+ SolidWorks CAD models and led plasma cutting and welding of Phase 2’s custom metal convection oven.

SolidWorks CADWeldingPlasma Cutting3D PrintingTeam Lead
Jan 2025 – Present · Aggies Create
Phase 1 — Shredder Assembly
Current · Academic

Mars Rover Simulation Engine

Solely responsible for 7 of 9 Phase 2 deliverables — built a complete Python rover simulation engine modeling rover dynamics ODEs, motor speed, mechanical power output, battery energy consumption, and full trajectory simulation. Also reverse-engineered and documented an undocumented prior codebase for full project handoff.

PythonODE ModelingNumerical MethodsSimulation
Jan 2026 – Present · Engineering Analysis Course
Academic

Mechanical Table Tennis Toy

Designed and assembled a crank-driven mechanical toy using Chebyshev lambda linkages to convert rotational motion into controlled linear motion. Performed kinematic analysis, gear ratio optimization, and tolerance refinement; executed laser cutting and 3D printing of the final design.

SolidWorksKinematic AnalysisLaser Cutting3D Printing
Sep – Dec 2025 · Geometric Modeling Course
Finished trophy with laser-engraved A&M base
Personal · Business

RAD Builds™ – Prop Studio & YouTube

Founded and operated a fabrication business building movie props (Thor’s Stormbreaker, Attack on Titan ODM Gear, Iron Man helmet, Infinity Gauntlet) from foam, wood, PVC, and resin. Produced instructional YouTube videos for 1,000+ subscribers and fulfilled custom merchandise orders for clients ranging from local bands to cruise groups.

FabricationCAD / OnshapeVideo ProductionEntrepreneurship
Aug 2022 – Present · youtube.com/@radbuilds3000
Ryan building at his workbench
Personal

Infinity War Mural with LED Lighting

Painted a large-scale Marvel Infinity War mural featuring Thanos and Iron Man scaled to wall dimensions, with an integrated remote-controlled LED strip lighting system. Created as the backdrop for RAD Builds YouTube content.

Mural PaintingLED ElectronicsDesign
Jan – Feb 2024
Thanos vs Iron Man mural with LED frame
Academic

DC Motor – From Scratch

Wound and assembled a fully functional DC motor from copper coils and a handmade wood frame, demonstrating fundamental electromagnetic and mechanical engineering principles.

ElectricalFabricationCoil Winding
